commands.review_summary:
The PR filters replay-unsafe `sessions_send` branch entries out of compaction-safeguard fallback history and adds focused regression tests for source- and target-side send artifacts.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +148. Total +255 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. not as a live workflow: the source path and new regression cases are clear, but no current-main or after-fix two-agent gateway run was established for the timing-dependent duplicate.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Wait for contributor live proof or a maintainer proof override; there is no narrow automated repair because the patch itself has no confirmed code defect.

target.title: fix(compaction): skip replay-unsafe session sends
target.body:
Fixes #84139.

## Summary

- Filters replay-unsafe `sessions_send` branch entries before the compaction safeguard uses session-branch fallback history.
- Keeps the existing visible-branch fallback for ordinary custom/user/tool history so empty-preparation compaction still writes a boundary instead of re-entering the empty compaction loop.
- Adds focused regressions for both sides of the duplicate path: the target session's inter-session input turn and the source session's already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source invariant for the compaction safeguard branch fallback is that it may recover real conversation content only when the recovered branch entries are safe to summarize as prior history. Before this patch, `collectSessionBranchMessages()` fed the fallback path every branch entry because empty compaction preparation had lost the normal message set; that treated side-effecting `sessions_send` delivery artifacts as ordinary conversation and let compaction recovery reintroduce already-executed inter-session send turns.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix is at the branch-fallback source boundary, before `containsRealConversation()` and summarization decide whether recovered branch content is usable. It removes only replay-unsafe messaging send artifacts from that recovered branch: target-session `inter_session` user inputs whose source tool is `sessions_send`, their immediate assistant/tool reply, and source-session messaging send tool calls/results. Ordinary visible custom branch entries, normal user/assistant history, and non-messaging tool results continue through the existing fallback path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The added fallback wording is scoped to the existing compaction safeguard branch fallback path, not a new runtime fallback. The filter preserves the prior empty-boundary guard for no-real-content compaction and avoids broad catch/default behavior changes.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth contract for replay-unsafe messaging send detection remains `isMessagingToolSendAction()`. The compaction safeguard now reuses that same contract instead of special-casing only transcript text, while `inputProvenance` remains the canonical marker for delivered inter-session user inputs.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** No config, schema, API, migration, or default compaction settings changed. Existing ordinary branch fallback behavior remains covered by the existing test that preserves visible custom branch entries and read tool results.
- **Related open PR scan:** The recorded related open PR scan reported `related_open_prs: []` and `scan_error: null`, so this is not competing with a broader same-contract PR found by the daily-fix scan.
- **Out of scope:** Live gateway PM-to-bee session reproduction, unrelated stale messaging-tool dedupe state, provider summarizer behavior, session storage format, and non-`sessions_send` product semantics are intentionally not changed.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:stateful-runtime` and `live-proof-not-run` style risk were considered because the bug is in compaction/session recovery behavior and the exact multi-agent live run was not executed.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes only which recovered branch entries are allowed into compaction fallback summarization after compaction preparation omitted real content. It does not change delivery, idempotency keys, session storage, or the normal compaction preparation path.
- **Why acceptable:** The focused regressions exercise the exact branch-fallback seam for both source and target `sessions_send` artifacts, and the acceptance suite covers compaction overflow plus sessions_send/A2A behavior after the patch.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Prevents compaction safeguard branch fallback from reusing already-executed `sessions_send` interactions as fallback history during compaction recovery, while preserving ordinary visible branch history fallback.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw issue worktree at `/media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-84139` using the repository's `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` runner.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  194 passed (194)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 33.76s
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compaction safeguard test suite now includes regressions proving target-session `sessions_send` inter-session turns produce a minimal no-prior-history boundary instead of branch summarization, and source-session `sessions_send` tool calls/results are removed from recovered branch history while the initiating user request remains summarizable.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live PM session sending to a bee/child session through a real gateway UI, nor external channel/provider live proof. Remote CI and ClawSweeper review are not included in this local proof.

## Regression coverage

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Empty compaction preparation with session branch fallback now filters target-side `inter_session` inputs from `sessions_send` and source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result entries.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression sits at the branch-fallback seam that produced the duplicate recovery behavior, without requiring a full multi-agent live run or changing sessions_send delivery code.
